highlight: Six new countries join the calculator, bringing total coverage to 27 countries with 2026 tax data for all major destinations.
improvements: The country configuration panel now opens as a full-height side sheet on desktop and a bottom drawer on mobile, making it much easier to set up a destination on a phone without the keyboard covering inputs
Tax notices and warnings in the configuration panel are now shown inline with expandable detail, so you can read the full context without losing your place in the form
Official tax sources and methodology notes for each country are now accessible directly from the configuration panel, letting you verify the numbers behind a calculation
A bug report button has been added to the calculator toolbar, pre-filling a GitHub issue with the current page URL so you can flag calculation errors in seconds
Landing page preset cards now link directly to the correct calculator configuration, including regional variants such as the Dutch 30% Ruling in the Expat Havens preset

Portugal 2026 now correctly applies the mandatory specific employment income deduction (dedução específica) — the greater of €4,104 or 11% of gross — before computing IRS, matching how Portuguese tax law actually works
Italy 2026 now models the INPS contribution cut (cuneo fiscale), which reduces employee social security rates for lower and middle earners in line with 2026 legislation
fixes: Corrected the Spain Beckham Law calculation: the personal allowance credit and intermediate deduction node are now properly removed under the flat-rate regime, so net income figures match the actual 24%/47% structure
